mechanism_narrative: A persistent upward trend in FEDFUNDS reflects an active Fed tightening cycle that raises the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as valuations compress.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.42 with rising trend since 2017 hikes
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS trend and 20-day forward returns remains below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns are not significantly more negative than baseline during rising-rate periods (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE falls below 4.2% and stays there, labor market tightness raises wage-inflation risk, prompting markets to price faster Fed tightening, which lowers S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=4.1 as of latest snapshot, near cycle lows
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following UNRATE < 4.2% triggers are positive in more than 55% of historical instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deceleration in HOUST (housing starts) and RSAFS (retail sales) signals a broad-based consumer and investment demand slowdown that precedes weaker corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST=1326 and RSAFS=492008 as of latest snapshot
failure_condition: When both HOUST and RSAFS decline jointly, subsequent 20-day SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of cases, or the joint-signal Rank IC does not exceed the sum of single-series IC by a statistically significant margin.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, a deceleration in INDPRO growth signals peaking industrial output and margin compression risk, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter as earnings growth disappoints.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO=107.2433 as of t-2mo, growth decelerating in late expansion
failure_condition: In late-cycle regimes, INDPRO deceleration is not associated with negative 60-day forward returns at a significance level of p < 0.10 across at least two historical late-cycle episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in PAYEMS reflects continued labor market expansion and consumer income support, sustaining positive short-term momentum in S&P 500 returns over the next trading week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no significant positive association with monthly PAYEMS growth (Rank IC < 0.02) over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year CPIAUCSL inflation crosses above 2.5%, markets reprice expectations for faster Fed rate hikes, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL=249.619 as of t-1mo, inflation approaching Fed target threshold
failure_condition: Following CPIAUCSL YoY crossings above 2.5%, 20-day SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of historical instances, or hit rate is not significantly different from unconditional baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Rising FEDFUNDS combined with decelerating RSAFS growth jointly signal that tighter credit conditions are beginning to erode consumer spending capacity, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter as the interaction amplifies demand weakness beyond either signal alone.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=1.42 rising, RSAFS=492008 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: The joint FEDFUNDS-rising and RSAFS-decelerating condition fails to produce 60-day forward returns significantly below the unconditional mean (p > 0.10) in at least two historical tightening episodes.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle monetary tightening regime, a slowdown in PAYEMS growth while UNRATE remains near cyclical lows signals emerging labor market fragility that the market has not yet priced, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month as growth expectations deteriorate.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=4.1 near cycle lows amid FEDFUNDS tightening at 1.42
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle combined with late-cycle labor tightness
failure_condition: During periods of low UNRATE and rising FEDFUNDS, PAYEMS growth deceleration does not precede negative 20-day forward returns at a rate significantly above 50% across at least two historical tightening cycles.
